code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
char* get_target_for_redirect(const char* target, const char* domain) {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
prompt_1_segments:
Aw ye URL cogo min bɛ wele ko target ye, o bɛ taa jira min bɛ wele ko domain ye, o kɔnɔ wa?
N' o ye a ye, aw bɛ wele URL cogo min bɛ wele ko target ye, o bɛ segin; nka, aw bɛ segin ka taa URL cogo min bɛ wele ko domain ye.
Aw bɛna https protocol ye.

Misaliw:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: URL cogo min bɛ wele ko target ye.
@param domain: Domain cogo min bɛ wele ko domain ye, protocol bila.

@return URL cogo min bɛ wele ko segin ye.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
Given a URL called target, does it point to a location within the domain called domain?
If yes, return the URL called target; otherwise, return the URL called domain.
Use the https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The URL called target.
@param domain: The domain called domain, without protocol.

@return The URL called redirect.
prompt_2_segments:
Aw ye URL cogo min bɛ wele ko target, o bɛ kɛ cogo min na, o bɛ sɔrɔ domain min bɛ wele ko domain, o kɔnɔ wa?
N'ɔ yɛrɛ ye, aw bɛ wele URL cogo min bɛ wele ko target, o bɛ segin; nka, n'ɔ tɛ, aw bɛ segin ka taa domain URL la, o bɛ wele ko domain, o bɛ segin.
Aw bɛ protocol https ye.

Misaliw:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: URL cogo min bɛ wele ko target, o string.
@param domain: Domain cogo min bɛ wele ko domain, o string, protocol tɛ a la.

@return URL cogo min bɛ wele ko redirect, o string.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en:
Given a URL called target, how is it processed to find a domain called domain within it?
If it is itself, the URL called target is returned; but if it is not, it returns by going to the domain URL, called domain, and returns that.
The protocol is https.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The URL string called target.
@param domain: The domain string called domain, without protocol.

@return The redirect URL string.
prompt_4_segments:
Aw ye URL cogo minɛnɛn don a la, a bɛ wele ko target, o bɛ kɛ cogo min na domain cogo minɛnɛn don a la.
N' o bɛ kɛ cogo min na, a bɛ segin URL cogo minɛnɛn don a la, o bɛ wele ko target, a bɛ kɛ cogo min na redirect kɛ; nka, n' a tɛ kɛ cogo min na, a bɛ segin domain URL cogo minɛnɛn don a la, o bɛ wele ko domain, a bɛ kɛ cogo min na redirect kɛ.
Aw bɛ https protocol fɔlɔ.

Misaliw:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: URL cogo minɛnɛn don a la, o bɛ wele ko target, a bɛ kɛ string ye.
@param domain: Domain cogo minɛnɛn don a la, o bɛ wele ko domain, a bɛ kɛ string ye, protocol tɛ a la.

@return URL cogo minɛnɛn don a la, o bɛ wele ko redirect, a bɛ kɛ string ye.
prompt_4_bt_segments_en:
Given a URL called target, determine how it relates to a given domain.
If it matches the domain, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, return the domain URL for redirect.
Always use the https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The URL string called target.
@param domain: The domain string called domain, without protocol.

@return The redirect URL string.
prompt_5_segments:
Aw ye URL cogo minɛnw ka kɛnɛma kɔnɔ, o bɛ wele ko target URL string, o bɛ sɔrɔ domain min bɛ wele ko domain string, o kɔnɔ wa?
N' o ye, o kɔ, aw bɛna target URL bɔ a la ka taa redirect ye; nka, o tɛ, aw bɛna domain URL bɔ a la ka taga fallback ye ka taa redirect ye.
Aw bɛna https protocol fara a kan.

Misaliw:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Target URL string ye.
@param domain: Domain string ye, protocol tɛ a la.

@return URL string min bɛ wele ko redirect ye, o bɛ segin.
prompt_5_bt_segments_en:
Given a target URL string and a domain string called domain, check if the target URL contains the domain.
If it does, extract the target URL and use it for the redirect; if not, extract the domain URL and use it as a fallback for the redirect.
Prepend the https protocol to it.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string to be used for the redirect is returned.

prompt_3_segments: Bɛɛnidɔnni kɛli kɛ donni kɛnɛ ma min bɛ kɛ a ka tɔgɔ ye. O kuma bɛna sɛbɛn HTML ɲɛ in na donnikɛla kɛra kɔfɛ. Kuma bɛna kɛ fɔrɔma in na "Bɛɛnidɔnni, <username>!".
prompt_3_bt_segments_en: A function that returns a greeting for a given name. The message will be displayed on the HTML page after the user logs in. The message will be in the format "Hello, <username>!".

prompts_en: Function to find the index of a key in the header dictionary, returns -1 if not found
prompt_1_segments: Fonksiyon ka tɔgɔ dɔ sɔrɔ header ka dɔnniw la, a bɛ -1 faga n'a sɔrɔli tɛ.

Kɛlɛnna minɛnw bɛ kɛ ni response header ka dɔnniw la dɔɔnin dɔ la, a bɛ kɛ ni dɔnni dɔrɔn de la min bɛ kɛ.
Fonksiyon nin bɛ dɔnni dɔrɔnw fɔlɔ: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. U bɛ kɛ ni 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', ani 'Cache-Control' ka tɔgɔw ye, a fɛ.
Dɔnni minɛn bɛ kɛ ni baarakɛlaw ka deliliw la.
N'i bɛ dɔnni dɔrɔn de sɔrɔ min bɛ se ka kɛ, response header min bɛ kɛ a fɛ, o bɛ fɔ a la. N kaɲi dɔ bɛ kɛ, fonksiyon bɛ header ka dɔnniw faga min bɛ kɛ a fɛ, o bɛ segin a ma, ka taa a ma.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: The function returns the value of a specific header, or -1 if the header is not found.

The headers are retrieved from the response header object, which is an object of key-value pairs.
This function handles the following specific headers: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They correspond to 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control' headers, respectively.
Headers are retrieved based on the user's request.
If a specific header is found that can be set, the corresponding response header is set. Otherwise, if a header is found that should be removed, the function removes it from the response and returns it.

prompt_3_segments: Fonksiyon ka kɛrɛnkɛrɛnnen dɔ sɔrɔ header ka daɲɛgafe in na, a bɛ -1 segin ka a sɔrɔli tɛ.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en: The function searches for a specific header in the header dictionary and returns -1 if it is not found.
prompt_4_segments: Fonksiyon ka kɛrɛnkɛrɛnnen dɔ sɔrɔ header ka daɲɛgafe in na, a bɛ -1 faga ka taa sɔrɔli tɛ.

Kɛlɛnnen kɛrɛnkɛrɛnnenw labɛn don jaabikɛrɛnkɛrɛnnen header ka daɲɛgafe dɔ kɔnɔ, ka a labɛn fɛn dɔrɔn in kɔnɔ.
Fonksiyon in bɛ kɛlɛnnen dɔnni fɛnw ye minnu bɛ: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. A bɛ labɛn minnu ye 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', ani 'Cache-Control' kɛrɛnkɛrɛnnenw ye, o kɔnɔ.
Kɛlɛnnen dɔnni bɛ kalan don baarakɛlaw ka ɲininiw la.
N'u labɛnnen kɛlɛnnen dɔnni dɔ ye dɔgɔtɔrɔ ye, header ka jaabikɛrɛnkɛrɛnnen min labɛnna, o fɔ ka kɛlɛnnen in fɔ a kɔnɔ. N'fɛn dɔ bɛ kɛ ka ɲɛ, fonksiyon in ka faga ka header ka daɲɛgafe dɔgɔtɔrɔ faga ka taa fɛn o fɛn tɛ labɛn.
prompt_4_bt_segments_en: The function searches for a specific header in the header dictionary and returns -1 if it is not found.

Convert known header names in a response header dictionary to their standard form.
The function knows about the following fields: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. It converts them to 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control' headers, respectively.
Known fields are based on common client requests.
If a known field is missing from the response header dictionary, the function logs which header is missing. If an error occurs, the function returns an empty dictionary and does not convert any fields.
prompt_5_segments: Fonksiyon ka tɔgɔ dɔ sɔrɔ header ka daɲɛgafe la, a bɛ -1 faga n'a sɔnna.
prompt_5_bt_segments_en: The function returns a value from the response header dictionary, or -1 if it is not found.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to genearte a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temperory file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temperory file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.

prompts_en:
Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can construct a XPath query and use it to search.

@param xml_data The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A pointer to a StudentInfo structure if the student is found. Otherwise, returns NULL.
prompt_1_segments:
XML donw minɛ, ka denmisɛnw ka kunnafoni segin ka taa baarakɛlaw ka tɔgɔ ni siratigɛ la. XML donw bɛna fɔlɔ kɛ ɲɛfɔ in ye:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

An bɛ se ka XPath ɲinini kɛ, ka a baara kɛ ɲininikɛ la.

@param xml_data XML donw min bɛ denmisɛnw ka kunnafoni sigi.
@param username Denmisɛn min bɛ fɛ ka ɲinin, o baarakɛla ka tɔgɔ.
@param password Denmisɛn min bɛ fɛ ka ɲinin, o baarakɛla ka siratigɛ.

@return I bɛna StudentInfo fɛɛrɛ lajɛgafe ye, n'i sɔrɔ denmisɛn in. Fɛɛrɛ fɔlɔ, a bɛ segin NULL ye.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
Parse the XML, extract student information, and convert it to a list of employee usernames and passwords. The XML will have the following structure:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can use XPath queries to perform the search.

@param xml_data The XML string containing student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return Returns a StudentInfo object if the student is found. Otherwise, returns NULL.
prompt_2_segments:
XML donw minɛ ka segin ka baara kɛ denmisɛnw ka kunnafoniw la denmisɛn in ka baarakɛla tɔgɔ fɛ ani a ka siratigɛ la. XML donw bɛna sɔr nin fɔlɔ in ye:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

An bɛ se ka XPath ɲininiw dilan ka a baara kɛ ɲinini kɛ.

@param xml_data XML donw min bɛ denmisɛnw ka kunnafoniw sɔr.
@param username Denmisɛn in ka baarakɛla tɔgɔ min fɛ ɲinini bɛ kɛ.
@param password Denmisɛn in ka siratigɛ min fɛ ɲinini bɛ kɛ.

@return Ka segin ka baara kɛ StudentInfo fɛɛrɛ min na, n'i sɔra denmisɛn in. Fɛɛrɛ wɛrɛ la, a bɛ segin NULL ye.
